sdd-fleet
  • Brief
  • Boundary
  • Architecture
    • Overview
    • Workspace
    • Feature machine
    • Memory
  • Provenance
  • Pilot
01

Memory: a linked vault, not a curated store

The estate has to remember its plans, specs, decisions and lessons. The trap is letting a model curate that memory, because that puts non-determinism in the foundation the next spec builds on. The structure already chosen supplies the answer: one Obsidian vault with two .sdd/ levels — workspace plans above, each repo's specs and ADRs below — all plain markdown, so a human and Claude Code open the identical files.

Every consequence lives in code; the model judges only the irreducible residue; a human owns the blast radius. Harness Model Human the one rule →

The record — authored markdown & links Harness-disciplined

Epic plans and contract designs in the workspace .sdd/; specs, acceptance and per-change ADRs in each repo's own .sdd/; cross-service lessons promoted up from repo to workspace level — all gated, git-versioned, changed only through review. Documents correlate through links written into the markdown, and because the repos are submodules of the one vault, those links cross repo boundaries. Each link is part of the record: reviewed in the PR, diffable, as auditable as the prose around it.

The index — derived backlinks & graph Derived · deterministic

Obsidian builds the backlink pane and the graph view from those authored links — nothing hand-kept, and it regenerates exactly from the record. Claude Code recovers the same neighbourhood by reading the links directly. Navigation is bidirectional, spans the whole workspace, and is a projection of recorded fact — never a model's recollection.

# workspace/  — ONE Obsidian vault; member repos are git submodules (pinned SHAs)
#   .sdd/               ← WORKSPACE level · epic plan · contract design · lessons
#   authoriser/ .sdd/   ← REPO level · spec.md · acceptance.md · ADR-217.md
# authoriser/.sdd/spec.md:  Implements [PAY-1843] · payments.authorise@3.3.0 · [acceptance](./acceptance.md)
# backlinks are computed from these forward links — never written by hand
Memory stays out of the model's hands. The vault is plain, diffable, git-versioned markdown that a human and Claude Code read identically; no model curates it, and a semantic index may sit on top for discovery but never feeds a gate.
02

The mechanic, borrowed from Obsidian

In Obsidian, you author forward links in your notes; the backlink pane and the graph view are derived — computed from those authored links, never hand-kept. The record is what you wrote; the index is a projection of it.

what sdd-fleet takes The vault is one Obsidian vault over the member repos, so memory is plain-markdown forward links — reviewed in the PR, diffable, as auditable as the prose around them — while the backlink graph is a derived projection a human and the agent recover identically. The discipline that falls out: standard markdown links, no [[wikilinks]] (they die on GitHub and to the gate parsers), cross-tree refs by stable ID, and a semantic index may sit on top for discovery but never feeds a gate. Memory is authored, never model-curated. Where this idea sits among the other three pillars: Four ideas, one boundary.
← PreviousLayer 2 — inside one repository Next →Four ideas, one boundary
sdd-fleet — a Claude Code plugin · engineered for audit and replay
Deterministic harness · probabilistic generation · human accountability
